channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Volleyball match with transgender player gets huge last-minute location change amid security controversies
item.description: San Jose State's upcoming game against the University of Nevada has been at the center of a national debate on transgender inclusion in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jack Flaherty will start for Dodgers in World Series opener against Gerrit Cole and Yankees
item.description: Jack Flaherty will start for the Los Angeles Dodgers in the World Series opener against the New York Yankees.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nestor Cortes expects to be on Yankees World Series roster, even if it's a long-term health risk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nestor Cortes expects to be on the New York Yankees' World Series roster against the Los Angeles Dodgers after throwing two innings of batting practice and pronouncing himself recovered from an elbow injury that has sidelined him for more than a month.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: MLB wants Rays to play home games in Tampa Bay area even if Tropicana Field isn't repaired in time
item.description: MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred says the league hopes the Tampa Bay Rays can play their home games in the area after Tropicana Field was decimated by Hurricane Milton
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vinicius Junior scores hat trick as Real Madrid beats Dortmund 5-2 in Champions League
item.description: Vinicius Junior scored a hat trick as Real Madrid turned boos into cheers with a 5-2 win over Borussia Dortmund in the Champions League on Tuesday, while Paris Saint-Germain dropped more points.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 76ers rule Embiid and George out for opener with injuries. Bucks' Middleton also expected to be out
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Paul George's debut with the Philadelphia 76ers is on hold and Joel Embiid is again set to miss time with an injury.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pulisic scores direct from corner kick for AC Milan. Then admits it was an accident
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Christian Pulisic scored direct from a corner kick to set AC Milan on the way to its first victory in this season's Champions League. But the United States international admitted it was an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stanley Cup champion Panthers and coach Paul Maurice agree on contract extension
item.description: SUNRISE, Fla. (AP) — The Florida Panthers have given out another big extension — this one, to their Stanley Cup-winning coach. Paul Maurice and the Panthers have agreed on an extended contract, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tickets now on sale for San Diego's 2025 Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: This will be San Diego's 73rd year hosting a PGA Tour event and the 58th time it's being held at Torrey Pines Golf Course.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Deion Sanders attacked by anti-religion group for Colorado team chaplain – legal expert says he can have one
item.description: Colorado football coach and NFL Hall of Famer Deion Sanders came under fire by an anti-religion group, the Freedom From Religion Foundation, for a team chaplain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What’s wrong with Arizona? Coach Brent Brennan searches for solutions as time slips away
item.description: Quarterback Noah Fifita is struggling, receiver Tetairoa McMillan is invisible and the losses are mounting in Tucson.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Finishing strong: Palo Alto’s resilient Sophie Pan has overcome sprained ankles, a broken finger and more
item.description: Palo Alto's senior volleyball standout Sophie Pan missed 22 matches this season, the latest in a long line of setbacks for the determined college-bound middle blocker.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Atletico trying to raise awareness of fans with disabilities in Champions League game
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Atletico Madrid wants to attract a record number of spectators with disabilities to a Champions League match to raise awareness about inclusion and the need to promote better access to sport for disabled fans in venues across Europe.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watkins, Bueckers headline preseason AP All-America team in women's hoops; 3 sophomores for 1st time
item.description: USC star JuJu Watkins and UConn’s Paige Bueckers headline The Associated Press preseason All-America team that for the first time includes three sophomores in a clear sign of the young talent in a sport coming off a record year of fan interest.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Legendary Yankees manager Joe Torre gives advice to Aaron Boone ahead of World Series
item.description: Aaron Boone is headed to his first World Series as a manager, and four-time champion Joe Torre is giving him some advice ahead of the Fall Classic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PPR Podcast #194: Brodie Stump, Kearny QB
item.description: All Kearny's Brodie Stump does is throw touchdown passes. That's why he's earned a center seat in PPR Podcast #194.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: After bye week, Bears hope to maintain momentum for upcoming key games
item.description: Given a week off after their offense got rolling against Jacksonville in London, Caleb Williams and the Chicago Bears might have been better served without a Week 7 bye.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WNBA players union decides to opt out of current collective bargaining agreement
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA players union has decided to opt out of the current collective bargaining agreement, two years before its expiration. The league and players union had the option to do so before Nov. 1. The early opt-out marks a crucial juncture for the league.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raiders QB Aidan O'Connell expected to be out 4-6 weeks with a broken thumb, AP source says
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — Las Vegas Raiders quarterback Aidan O'Connell is expected to be out four to six weeks with a broken thumb, a person close to the situation said Monday. The person spoke to The Associated Press on condition of anonymity because the Raiders have not made an announcement.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ticker-tape parade after NY Liberty win: Everything you need to know
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – After a massive win for the New York Liberty Sunday night, the city is ready to celebrate. On Thursday, New York City will host a ticker-tape parade for the team's first-ever WNBA championship title, Mayor Eric Adams announced. The parade will kick off at 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Ricky Pearsall gives 49ers a lift with his return. But they lose Brandon Aiyuk to a knee injury
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The San Francisco 49ers got a lift from rookie receiver Ricky Pearsall making his NFL debut exactly 50 days after being shot in the chest but might have lost starting receiver Brandon Aiyuk to a significant knee injury.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WNBA Finals reach Game 5 for first time since 2019, Liberty head back to New York
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It's fitting that a record-breaking season for the WNBA would come down to a winner-take-all Game 5 of its Finals. For the first time since 2019, the league championship will go the distance. This one will be in New York on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Christian Barmore apologizes to Rhode Island police after racism remarks
item.description: Patriots defensive tackle Christian Barmore issued an apology on social media to the Providence Police Department after he accused officers of racism during a recent traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Baccari, NewsNation

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Criticism sparked over Snapdragon Stadium field conditions
item.description: The San Diego Wave FC lost 1-0 to Mexico’s Club America Wednesday. Both teams are advancing to the semifinals, but it was the turf that was the talk of the tailgate.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Anaheim knocks off Utah in overtime, 5-4
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) - After winning two overtime games to begin its first road trip, the Utah Hockey Club closed out the trip with an overtime loss.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
